the sequence grows with a pattern that the prisoner can learn to understand
solving the recurrence requires finding roots of the characteristic equation first
the homogeneous part leads to a solution involving powers of two
the particular solution adds a constant to handle the extra two
combining these yields a closed form for the nth term of the sequence
the formula is \( a_n = (n+1)2^n \) which matches the initial conditions given
the prisoner now sees how mathematical rules create predictable growth paths
next the therapist introduces a cipher based on the famous Fibonacci numbers
the transformation rule is \( b_n = 3F_n + 2F_{n+1} \) for the encoded message
using the identity \( F_{n+1} = F_n + F_{n-1} \) helps simplify the expression
substituting this into the equation reveals a direct link to later terms
the result simplifies to \( b_n = 5F_{n+1} - 2F_n \) which is not fully reduced
wait a better approach uses \( F_{n+2} = F_{n+1} + F_n \) to find the true form
rewriting gives \( 3F_n + 2F_{n+1} = F_{n+1} + 2(F_n + F_{n+1}) = F_{n+1} + 2F_{n+2} \)
actually the simplest form is \( b_n = 5F_{n+1} - 2F_n \) or just calculate terms
the first five terms are 8 13 21 34 55 based on standard Fibonacci values
this shows the prisoner that complex codes often hide simple underlying structures
the method of solving these puzzles affects the way the mind processes new information
the prisoner can now apply these skills to plan for a better future outside
the therapeutic value of math lies in building confidence through logical success
the sequence and the cipher both demonstrate that order exists within chaos
the prisoner has successfully completed the challenge set by the dedicated therapist
the journey of rehabilitation is a sequence of small steps toward a big goal
the closed form and the simplified cipher are tools for the mind to grow
the prisoner understands that every term in a sequence has a place in the whole
